reviews.review_msg: Fabulous Prof! The most interesting and useful prof I've ever met. Teaches you the real world stuff, not those old theory concepts that dun even apply in today's world! A big lost for SFU for cutting budget on this Creativity in Bus course.  This course is what you need to improve the world and the world of Bus! Valuable course, strongly recommend!
I thought Ginger had a great teaching method. I would attend lectures just to hear her stories. In order to ace this class memorize the text and you'll be set!
I really enjoyed this course. The material is mainly memorizing the textbook but Ginger made it as interesting as she could.
Don't know what's wrong with previous comments - I found her excellent! Helpful and great attitude towards students. But, I am getting good marks in the course!

What can I say, Ginger is very condescending to the students in this class. She literally treats everyone like a child, assumes everyone doesn't know anything and couldn't care less about her students. I know she's a great prof in upper level courses, but it'll be great if her attitude was the same. Oh and memorize the textbook!
AMAZING! Don't leave SFU without taking her Creativity in Business course. This is her specialty - and it shows. Should be a required course in Business!
Enjoyed the course - she does the best she can with this material. Mandatory bell curve can make it a nightmare so be prepared!
She did not seem interested in teaching, only the subject matter and listening to herself speak.

Her classes are interesting and she is funny and refreshing....BUT the midterm and exam if entirely on the TEXTBOOK (The tests are written by the textbook company) and she barely teaches anything on it. So if you do take this class memorize the textbook!

Ginger is an amazing public speaker, she inspires and interests you immediately. Unfortunately, she rarely talked about the actual course topic, and the entire course had to be done through the textbook. Could have been an amazing class if Ginger had lectured on the topic.  Unfortunately instead it was boring with frustrating teamwork.

Ginger is a cook. First of all, all this class was was pure presenting. Every group presented every class and she didn't teach at all! Everything I learned (which was not much) was self-taught. Furthermore, she was very vague on what she expected. And we had to wait over a month for a grade on an assignment!

took this class because of the great things i heard about her...very disappointed. Group presentations every week, she barely speaks. Its a shame because she has great stories and experience and our $700 spent on this class would be better spent listening to her. Midterm/final easy,based on textbook...high averages though so watch out for the curve

I was very interested in this course; at the end, it was not what I was expecting. We watched movies all semester. She just added 3 more chapters for final on last week of semester. I don't think she and TA teach anything. I personally didn't learn anything from her class except watching several movies. Don't take her class...Also she always late.

Ok. Very interesting prof. Loves to talk about M&A's, and case-it's, and visual analytics, but not much on actual org. theory. Exams/midterms ENTIRELY textbook based, but none of that material covered in class. You still have to attend class though because your group may be called to present (expect only 1 pres. per half-semester).
She's good because she relates the material, but in all honesty I found the lectures that weren't movies to be a little boring, and a tad repetitious,

She is a one of a kinda professor.  Though in the beginning it may be a little of a shocker on how she conducts the class, but it the end you will be wondering why other profs don't teach the same way.  It may seem that you don't do much in the class, but you learn so much without you actually knowing it.  Well...she is an archetypal expert after a

Omg! if you can get her, you are lucky! you learn tons of different stuff you will  never know in your life if you go to her lecture. It's not mandatory, but you will find yourself attending her lecture at some points. It's interesting, different, and exciting! She's so open minded, helpful, and inspiring students!
She is so inspiring and definitely makes you look yourself in a different way.
Totally agree!!!    Best prof ever!!!! Way to go Ginger! :))
Totally agree with the last person. Do not leave SFU without taking her course. She helps you to find the inner-you!
BEST PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER MET! Knowledgeable and nice person!
